name: Easton Tantrum Series 13" Slow Pitch Softball Glove: ETNT130-6N
descriptionShort: A 13" slowpitch outfield glove with quality full-grain leather, a deep bucket pocket, and the length outfielders need to reach in the gap.
notes: Plan on 1–2 weeks of catch before it feels dialed in. Soft, fast close — forgiving for developing players and quick on the exchange. A genuine slowpitch outfield pattern, not a baseball pattern with a larger label.

name: Easton Tantrum Series 13" Slow Pitch Softball Glove: ETNT130-6R
descriptionShort: The Easton Tantrum Series — a mid-grade outfield pattern with the length and deep bucket pocket slowpitch outfielders rely on for fly-ball security.
notes: Minimal break-in — a few sessions of catch and it's ready for game play. One-handed close is natural right out of the box. The deep-bucket pocket and open wrist are what separate good slowpitch outfield gloves from repurposed baseball patterns.

name: Wilson A2000 13.5" Slow Pitch Softball Glove: WBW101648135
descriptionShort: A 13.5" slowpitch utility glove that covers the field — deep bucket pocket, H-web, and the flexibility to move between positions.
notes: Plan on 4–6 weeks of real break-in before it hits its stride. Expect real rigidity early on; that stiffness is what keeps the pocket from blowing out years later. Real slowpitch geometry, not a rebadged baseball pattern.

notes: Plan on 4–6 weeks of real break-in before it hits its stride. Runs stiff out of the box, which is the tradeoff for the long-term shape retention elite leather delivers. The deep-bucket pocket and open wrist are what separate good slowpitch outfield gloves from repurposed baseball patterns.
